name: Pino (Extremely Fast Node.js Logger)

performance.notes: 5x faster than Bunyan, 4x faster than Winston; minimal object creation; JSON serialization optimized

agent_readiness.notes: Fastest Node.js JSON logger. Structured logging with JSON output. Child loggers with inherited context. Asynchronous logging to minimize application impact. pino-pretty for human-readable dev output. Transport system: pino-elasticsearch, pino-loki, pino-opentelemetry. Fastify integration (native). Hapi, Express adapters. Log levels: trace, debug, info, warn, error, fatal. Redaction for sensitive fields. Used by Fastify natively.

gotchas: JSON output only (no custom formatters at core — use transports)
pino-pretty for dev output adds overhead (don't use in production)
Child logger context inheritance can be surprising
Asynchronous mode can lose logs on crash (sync mode available)
best_when: High-performance Node.js services requiring structured logging
Applications where logging is in the hot path
avoid_when: Applications needing complex log formatting (use Winston)
summary: Pino is the fastest Node.js JSON logger — 5x faster than Bunyan, 4x faster than Winston. Structured JSON output with child loggers, redaction, and async transport. pino-pretty for development. Transport system supports ELK, Loki, OpenTelemetry. Native Fastify integration. Best for high-performance Node.js services where logging overhead matters. Simple API with excellent performance characteristics.
